title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC  Network Changes

input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC  Network Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network changes.
input_row.rationale: It is possible to have more than one VPC within a project. In addition, it is also possible to create a peer connection between two VPCs enabling network traffic to route between VPCs. Monitoring changes to a VPC will help ensure VPC traffic flow is not getting impacted.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure the prescribed log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure at least one metric is present with filter text: resource.type="gce_network" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.removePeering" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.addPeering") Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/ stream is above a threshold of 0 for greater than 0 seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that appropriate notification channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure the log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with filter set to: resource.type="gce_network" AND protoPayload.methodName="beta.compute.networks.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName="beta.compute.networks.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName="v1.compute.networks.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName="v1.compute.networks.removePeering" OR protoPayload.methodName="v1.compute.networks.addPeering" 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/. Ensure the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains at least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="gce_network" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.removePeering" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.addPeering") 4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the user's advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the prescribed alert policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page appears. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of 0 for the most recent value will ensure that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 4. Configure the desired notification channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud logging metrics create Create the prescribed alert policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field has specific instructions on how to verify the control using both the Google Cloud Console and the gcloud CLI commands. 
- The mentioned commands ensure that the control can be fully verified through GCP APIs or Google Cloud SDK.
- There are clear references to log metrics and alert policies, which are standard service properties queryable via GCP APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_log_metric_filter_alerts_vpc_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Utilize the following GCP API calls and gcloud commands to verify the control:
  1. List the log metrics:
     - Command: `gcloud logging metrics list --format json`
     - This checks that there is at least one metric defined with the appropriate filtering criteria.
  2. Validate the filter of the existing log metrics:
     - Expected filter: 
       ```
       resource.type="gce_network" AND (
       protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.insert" OR
       protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.patch" OR
       protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.delete" OR
       protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.removePeering" OR
       protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.addPeering"
       )
       ```
     - Validate that the `metricDescriptor.type` starts with `logging.googleapis.com/user/`.
  3. List alerting policies:
     - Command: `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json`
     - This checks that there is at least one alerting policy configured for the identified log metric.
  4. Ensure the alerting policy meets the conditions:
     - Validate that:
       - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/"`.
       - `enabled` is set to `true`.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - Pass: If there is at least one log metric meeting the filter criteria and at least one alerting policy that is enabled with the correct conditions.
  - Fail: If either the log metric filter or the alerting policy is missing or does not meet the specified conditions.
step2_review:
**1. APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. The audit can be fully conducted using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), which allows for programmatically listing and validating log metrics and alerting policies.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:** The use of `gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` commands is appropriate for this automated check, leveraging the capabilities of the GCP CLI to access and validate the necessary configurations.

**2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are mostly correct. The commands listed are valid for checking the existence of log metrics and alerting policies. However, the use of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` could be updated to `gcloud monitoring policies list` if the command has graduated from alpha.
- **Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) Methods:** The methods and commands are valid, but the reliance on alpha commands should be minimized if possible.
- **Manual Steps:** Not applicable as the approach is automated.
- **Program Name:** The program name `gcp_logging_log_metric_filter_alerts_vpc_changes` is descriptive and clear, following the convention well.

**3. IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Missing Considerations:** Ensure that the `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` command is still necessary or if it has a stable version. If the command has graduated from alpha, update the documentation accordingly.
- **Alternative GCP API Methods:** Consider using the REST API directly for more complex checks or if additional data validation is required.
- **Better Program Name Suggestion:** The current program name is adequate, but if further specificity is needed, consider `gcp_logging_vpc_network_change_alerts`.

**4. CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- I am confident in the assessment's correctness and the ability to automate the audit using the specified GCP CLI commands.

**5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Update the use of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` to the stable version if available. This will ensure the audit approach remains robust and less dependent on alpha features.
- The rest of the assessment is accurate and should be maintained as is.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit can be automated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK. The necessary log metrics and alerting policies can be programmatically verified using `gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud monitoring policies list` commands. These commands allow for checking the existence and correctness of the log metric filters and alert policies, making manual inspection unnecessary.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_log_metric_filter_alerts_vpc_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Logging and Monitoring
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - `gcloud logging metrics list --format json`
  - `gcloud monitoring policies list --format json`
- **Properties to Check:** 
  - For log metrics: Ensure a metric exists with the filter `resource.type="gce_network" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.delete" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.removePeering" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.networks.addPeering")`.
  - For alert policies: Ensure at least one alert policy is associated with the log metric.
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - A log metric with the specified filter is present.
  - An alert policy linked to the log metric exists.
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - Absence of the log metric with the specified filter.
  - No alert policy associated with the log metric.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported automation. The decision to use `gcloud monitoring policies list` instead of the alpha version was made to ensure stability and reliability.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
